description: Netlify is a cloud platform that automates the build, deployment, and hosting of static and Jamstack sites, providing features such as continuous deployment from Git, serverless functions, form handling, and CDN distribution.
authentication: Supports OAuth via Git providers (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket) for site deployment and API access; API keys can be generated in the Netlify UI for programmatic access.
self_serve: Yes – users can sign up, connect a Git repository, and deploy sites directly from the Netlify dashboard without requiring a sales or support interaction.
api_surface: Public REST API for site management, build triggers, deploy previews, and function deployments; GraphQL API is also available for advanced queries.
mcp: Netlify’s Managed Continuous Deployment (MCD) model, where the platform handles build, deployment, and hosting, is the primary managed service offering.
buildability: Builds are triggered automatically on Git pushes; supports custom build commands, environment variables, and build plugins; integrates with various static site generators and frameworks.
main_blocker: Limited to static or serverless architectures; dynamic server-side rendering requires serverless functions or external services, which may increase complexity.
